# Screenshot And Demo Video Shot List

## Store Screenshots

Prepare 6-8 screenshots. Use English Revit UI if possible for global Store listing.

| # | Screenshot | Purpose | Status |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 1 | Revit ribbon with BIM Command Center tab | Proves Revit integration | Needed |
| 2 | Dockable dashboard open inside Revit | Main product identity | Needed |
| 3 | Model Health dashboard result | BIM manager value | Needed |
| 4 | Workset Dashboard view | Coordination value | Needed |
| 5 | MEP Splitter workflow | MEP productivity value | Needed |
| 6 | Clash section box or marker workflow | Coordination workflow | Needed |
| 7 | Clash report dashboard/capture workflow | Reporting value | Optional |
| 8 | Subscription check dialog without private data | Store entitlement clarity | Optional |

## Screenshot Rules

- Use a non-confidential sample model.
- Remove customer names, project names, coordinates and private paths.
- Avoid showing personal Autodesk account email.
- Prefer 1920x1080 or higher.
- Keep UI zoom readable.
- Do not show `RevitAddin.MepBim` or excluded AI/chat tools.

## Demo Video Script

Target length: 60-120 seconds.

1. Start with Revit open on a sample model.
2. Show the BIM Command Center ribbon tab.
3. Open the dockable dashboard.
4. Run or open Model Health.
5. Open Workset Dashboard.
6. Demonstrate one MEP Splitter workflow on copied elements.
7. Show a clash marker or section box workflow.
8. End on support/about/subscription status if available.

## Voiceover Draft

BIM Command Center for Revit brings daily BIM coordination tools into one Revit dashboard. BIM managers and MEP teams can access model health, workset review, auto-save support, MEP splitting tools and clash coordination utilities from a single command center. The first release focuses on local Revit productivity workflows with Autodesk App Store subscription entitlement.
